description: Automatically detect the start and end of vehicle trips by monitoring movement patterns across consecutive position records, applying configurable distance and duration thresholds to filter noise, ...

actors.name: Position Processing Pipeline
actors.description: Evaluates each position for motion state transitions using a rolling distance and duration model

actors.name: Fleet User
actors.description: Views trip history, distances, durations, and per-trip driver or fuel data in reports

fields.name: motion_streak
fields.label: Persisted flag indicating the device is currently in a motion (trip-in-progress) state

fields.name: motion_start_time
fields.label: Timestamp when the current motion episode began

fields.name: motion_start_latitude
fields.label: Coordinate where motion was first detected for the current trip

fields.name: motion_start_longitude
fields.label: Coordinate where motion was first detected for the current trip

fields.name: min_trip_distance_meters
fields.label: Minimum total distance a movement must cover to be classified as a trip (filters GPS jitter)

fields.name: min_trip_duration_seconds
fields.label: Minimum duration a movement episode must last to be classified as a trip

fields.name: stop_gap_seconds
fields.label: Seconds of stillness required to conclude that a trip has ended

values.label: Stopped
values.description: Device is stationary; no active trip

values.label: Moving
values.description: Device is in motion; a trip is in progress

transitions.description: Device movement detected after stationary period
Device has been stationary for stop_gap_seconds

rules.rule_1: Movement is detected when the device travels at least min_trip_distance_meters from the last recorded stop position
rules.rule_2: A trip is only recorded if the movement episode lasts at least min_trip_duration_seconds; shorter movements are treated as noise
rules.rule_3: A trip ends when the device remains stationary (below the motion speed threshold) for at least stop_gap_seconds
rules.rule_4: Motion state is persisted on the device record so it survives server restarts; the last known state is used as the baseline for the next incoming position
rules.rule_5: Only latest positions drive state transitions; outdated or replayed positions are ignored
rules.rule_6: Trip start and end positions are stored with full coordinates, timestamps, addresses, and cumulative sensor values (fuel, odometer, engine hours)
rules.rule_7: If ignition data is available, the ignition-off event can be used as a supplemental trip-end signal

given: device has moved >= min_trip_distance_meters from the last stop

then.description: Trip start event recorded with type = device_moving

trip_started.result: Trip start event stored; trip record begins accumulating distance and duration

given: device has been stationary for >= stop_gap_seconds

then.description: Trip end event recorded with type = device_stopped

trip_ended.result: Trip record completed with start/end coordinates, total distance, and duration

given: device moved but distance or duration did not meet minimum thresholds
noise_filtered.result: No trip events generated; movement treated as GPS jitter or brief repositioning

errors.message: The device referenced does not exist

events.name: trip.started
events.description: A new vehicle trip has begun

related.reason: Position data drives motion state transitions
Stop detection is the counterpart that classifies stationary periods between trips
Ignition events provide supplemental trip boundary signals
Trips are attributed to the identified driver for reporting
Trip records feed into scheduled trip reports

goals.description: Automatically detect the start and end of vehicle trips by monitoring movement patterns across consecutive position records, applying configurable distance and duration thresholds to filter noise, ...

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: workflow steps must complete correctly before proceeding
